mission.title: opentofu: Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to module configuration
mission.description: Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to module configuration. Requested by 150+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentofu setup
steps.description:
Verify your opentofu version and configuration:
```bash
tofu version
```
This feature requires a working opentofu installation.

steps.title: Review opentofu configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant opentofu configuration:
Many users expect to be able to DRY up their large configurations by using locals/variables in module sources and backend configurations (among other places).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to…
steps.description:
Yes that would be the largest functional difference on the CLI side.  Defaults could still be used if a variable is no passed in via -var or tfvars.  If a default is not given and it is not specified it would be considered missing and result in an error.

We would handle it the same way we handle someone editing the TF files between init and plan.  We would provide them with an error about what changed between init/plan.  If that is insufficient, we could track the values provided as vars to

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Yes that would be the largest functional difference on the CLI side.  Defaults could still be used if a variable is no passed in via -var or tfvars.  If a default is not given and it is not specified it would be considered missing and result in an error.

We would handle it the same way we handle someone editing the TF files between init and plan.

prerequisites.description: A working opentofu installation or development environment.
